item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am haunted by the possibility that the show really was real, just terribly obscure, and that the Foundation just destroyed an innocent fan's passion in their quest to contain anomalies.

With the Foundation's resources and wealth of knowledge and surveillance, it's almost certain that the show and its metafiction were anomalous. Yet I can't help but feel a cold hollowness when I read that final MTF comment.

Way to capture some uncomfortably relatable feelings. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In which the Foundation bullies an anomaly to death.

+1

item.title: Re: Batshit Insane Analysis
item.content:encoded:
My theory falls apart

it's incredibly notable at what time that post was written and where I put my attention

item.title: Re: Batshit Insane Analysis
item.content:encoded:
I…

You've somehow managed to overthink it and underthink it.

discrepancy in hair colour

At the risk of destroying the analysis, it's skin color.

item.title: Batshit Insane Analysis
item.content:encoded:
And probably one that's already been done.

There's a bunch of repeating numbers in the article.

9, 2, 11, and 7 I've seen.

The constant presence of those numbers has made me look into all possible meanings I could find.

+ show block
– hide block

Pasting the alphabetic values of W, A, V, and E brought me up with 51, and a reference to some psalm.

For Incident 14, Iridium stands at the 9th group of the atomic table. Iridium is in a romantic relationship that is a total of 2 people. If you stretch it horribly, you get 119. Iridium also has a 191 isotope. I'm pretty sure the discrepancy in hair colour has something to do with this.

Incident 66 talks about a certain Storm Sword. S is the 19th character in the alphabet. One character wielding the Storm Sword would make 119.

Incident 89 talks about episode 1 in season 9.

Now, for even more stretching on numbers.

5 is also recurrent. It is the total number of examples, and the sum of 1 + 4, 14 being the initial incident.

5 + 7 is 12, as is 6 + 6. Incident numbers.

1 + 1 + 9 is 11, leading me to believe there's something missing?

Penumbra WAVE had FOUR seasons, and went into hiatus
Our character had FIVE exemplified incidents before going into hiatus…? Surely missing something in here. 5 + 4 = 9.

The last incident fails to mention a central substantive like the others. First incident could be Iridium, second probably the porpoise, third is the storm sword, fourth is probably Angel.

Something in the back of my mind and apparently 2747's article now that I reread it reminds me about the Anafabula. If every attempt was imperfect, what if the last one was, and managed to replicate the properties of the Anafabula, deleting itself permanently?


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

Also, the beggining of the article refers to I/O-NYSE, which besides being another link to atoms and the like for me to waste time on, links back to Radiohead's album that was affected by 2747. There's something else in the structure that just makes me think this is a "simpler" 2747.

In retrospective, all of this sounds like the worst kind of conspiracy theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No one cares.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP. "No one cares" has so much finality to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to downvote because of how bad you treated the guy with the last log, but considering what it made me feel….+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Shitpost, Criticize, Peeve" ;-)

I love it! Kinda feel bad for the little skipper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand this is necroposting, but I disagree with adding more character to the SCP entity itself. The few things we know about the entity (That it only wants to contribute to a fandom of something it enjoys and is receiving nothing but hate) plays well into the experiences and fears for many who use social media and especially this site. Its this very lack of info that meets a very relatable situation/feelings (in this case, being attacked online constantly, not the idea of a shadow organization out to throw hate at your specifically) that give it the punch to the reader. Its like the nightmare of everyone who's been a fan of something- an entire group of people who are outright hostile to you and everything you do and constantly scours the internet just to shit on you and your every attempt to be part of the 'group'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ouch. I've been there, sad anime skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suicide is a bit far. I think there's a good chance that someone would sooner stop creating accounts and take an indefinite break from their source of stress than off themselves.

item.title: N E U T R A L I Z E D
item.content:encoded: Shitposting Foundation, what's not to +1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP, mostly because it mirrors some very early internet behavior of myself (post about something, get embarassed when it was called shit and remove it), which makes it a rather relatable SCP.

What makes this intriguing to me is that it's not clear if this is just an anomaly posting these comments or if the comments are the anomaly themselves. Both seem to be a possibility, although to me the former would be much more interesting to me.

Definetly a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3949 exists in a constant cycle of deleting itself and popping up again, even without Foundation involvement. All the MTF does is get 3949 posts taken down quicker than they would normally, to limit exposure.

The 119th incident's response was a little harsher than usual, sure, but they weren't trying to neutralize the skip.

Think of having to constantly shoo away pigeons from your yard, and then one day the pigeons just unexpectedly stop showing up for good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A comment from the Korean branch.

jso9923 :
It seems that an MTF neutralized a normal SCP… Isn't it against the mission of the Foundation? Why are they neutralizing an SCP, rather than protecting it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure, go for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that's why I'm asking first

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you can make me feel bad for a non-existent incorporeal entity, then you deserve an upvote

(Poor thing wasn't even doing anything wrong, just trying to make content for its favorite show, I can relate)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say, I'm skeptical such a plot twist would enhance the skip, but if Weryllium is game…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it ok if in the story I make the plot twist that the scp isn't a person? like its a computer in another dimension that can connect to internets of other dimensions? so they cant even contain it cause its not even in their dimension?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I most certainly don't mind :) Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this so much! I think I'm gonna use this for a story, do you mind if I add a little plot twist in the story?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah I just really like that number and didn't notice that I had snuck it in twice.

To clarify: lmaporpoise119 is not the same as the entity that is making all these posts. The art was a repost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all: +1, because that's a good skip.
Secondly: While it made me chuckle at the begining it genuinely made me sad.

Now, there's a thing with me overanalyzing things and seeking things athat aren't there, but does the anomaly's username: "lmaporpoise119" have to do anything with the total number of posts made by it: 119.

Probably just coincidence, but maybe not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why people say it is "funny" or "hilarious" is beyond me. It's almost heartbreakingly sad. This poor anomaly has been driven to suicide, ffs.

+1

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: wow. Congrats, dude! :D The SCP of the month

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favourite SCP reading.
Apparently, people do care :) Thanks for reading, everyone!
Apparently so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last comment is a little too real.

item.title: My first contribution on the SCP wiki is...
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting this SCP. This is an absolutely hilarious entry that still manages to be somewhat bizarre, with the song of the AMV being a fake song that sounds authentic.

Also, the end was truly something.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. I never thought the Foundation would resort to cyberbullying an SCP until it quit by self-neutralizing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This is so sad. Can we hit 200 upvotes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I didn't get "hilarious" or even "funny," from this, for me it was all heartbreak. Poor multiversal Marvin just wants to gush about his favorite show. I mean, it makes perfect sense in-universe. The Foundation needs to contain anomalies, this is an anomaly, then they sort of crossed the line and accidentally caused it to an hero.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how anticlimactic the ending was. The skip pours their heart out for their audience, and all they get is "No one cares." Fucking hilarious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oof, this one hurts. Way to make me sympathize with the thing, asshole.

…+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the omnitalented Kris Straub, author and illustrator of Starslip Crisis and Broodhallow and cocreator of Blamamation (with Scott Kurtz of PvP) as well as the gag strip Chainsawsuit and the gaming podcast 28 plays later. He does a horror fiction analysis podcast with Abby Howard (The Last Halloween) called "Scared Yet?" and generally has good things to say about us.

"Candle Cove" was part of his Ichor Falls project.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To put it shortly, it's a creepypasta in the form of a forum thread discussing a fake, horrifying children's show, with the posters gradually remembering all the horrible things about the show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't far from excellent. +1. I also want Penumbra W.A.V.E. to be a real thing now. Somebody get on that shit!

No one cares.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still know absolutely nothing about Candle Cove except that it's a creepypasta of some kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NYS

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it!

I flipflopped between Euclid and Keter but ultimately decided against Keter as I felt the SCP was already very nearly self-containing. All the normal people forget about Penumbra W.A.V.E. upon deletion of the primary account each time, which makes cleanup much easier.

Though if enough people agree with Keter more, I'm fine changing it.

I like SCPs that make me feel sympathy for the SCP. It adds depth to my experience that it gets me emotionally relating to it on both sides. Incident 119 cemented my upvote because I'm also a sucker for containment/neutralizations that are unexpectedly simple but make perfect sense in retrospect.

The only nitpick I have is: before this was neutralized, wouldn't it be Keter instead of Euclid since it was technically uncontained? If it was "located on various social media websites, forums, and message boards" then it sounds like it was uncontained on the entire internet, and all the Foundation could do at the time was to respond whenever they found another instance in the wild.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YMMV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this cross "cold, not cruel?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These things tend to happen. Just vote according to your opinion and, if you feel like explaining your choice, explain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't find this terribly interesting?
Is there something I'm not getting, because this seems to be getting a lot of praise..?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yo did the Foundation just bully a skip to suicide?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No one cares

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of cuberbullying an SCP into submission is incredibly hilarious for some reason. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Just a thought, because I like disruptive head cannon. Is there a reason the Foundation feels confident this is just an isolated thing? It feels to me like a symptom of a larger problem. "Neutralizing" it before tracking it down could have some fun (bad) consequences.

To me, it seems like it's

  1. some minor anomalous kook or effect
  2. a sign that at least one TV show was incompletely wiped from reality
  3. a lonely expat from a close parellel
  4. an trans-universal hole in the internet

Except for the first one, the cause is a lot worse than the effect.
Congrats on a skip that is not only good, but holds up to over-analysis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a real temptation to post "Nobody cares."

…but most of us have got epic opuses on the back-burner. :)
Using yours gave the fragments a real connectivity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww :(

Definitely a plus one. But aww.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Getting real Candle Cove vibes from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Nice and simple, and it had the (presumably) desired effect of making me feel sorry for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am ready to shit on it with every last foul thought I have.

Incident # Post Content Response
039 A comment celebrating all the other fans of Penumbra W.A.V.E. Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I drew from secondhand experience when coming up with 3949's posts (and the responses), so I'm glad they feel real.
